item.title: TIME-trend and forecasting of the economic impact of inflammatory bowel disease in Catalonia: a population-based analysis.
item.description: Journal of medical economics - Few studies have assessed the distribution and temporal trends of healthcare expenditures related to inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). The aims of the study were to analyze the expenditure trends f...

item.title: Folate and global health review series, part 4: syntheses on folate and autoimmune diseases and skeletal outcomes.
item.description: Journal of global health - Review - Autoimmune diseases and bone density loss (osteopenia and osteoporosis) are chronic conditions of complex aetiology that affect diverse populations. Folate may be associated with a higher risk of t...

item.title: Influenza and herpes zoster vaccine effectiveness in autoimmune or immune-mediated diseases.
item.description: Journal of infection and public health - Individuals with autoimmune or immune mediated diseases (AID) are more susceptible to infections and often experience worse outcomes if infected, underscoring the importance of vaccination. However...

item.title: Association Between Socioeconomic Conditions and Biologic Prescriptions for Inflammatory Bowel Diseases in the Brazilian Public Healthcare System: An Ecological Study.
item.description: Pharmacoepidemiology and drug safety - Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD) are chronic conditions affecting millions of people globally. Brazil's public healthcare system provides free access to IBD medications. However, socioeconomic con...

item.title: Oral Delivery of Mesenchymal Stem Cell-Derived Extracellular Vesicles To Treat Intestinal Inflammation.
item.description: ACS applied materials & interfaces - Despite advances in therapy for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), current treatments are associated with poor clinical outcomes and systemic side effects. Mesenchymal stem cell-derived extracellula...

item.title: Epidemiology and clinical profile of inflammatory bowel disease in Kuwait: a nationwide tertiary care (SEEF-IBD) study.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - The prevalence of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) continues to rise globally, with notable shifts in its epidemiological patterns. However, data on characteristics of IBD in Kuwait remain limited....

item.title: Extreme temperature events and hospitalizations for 59 autoimmune diseases in Korea: A nationwide outcome-wide, time-stratified, case-crossover study.
item.description: Environmental research - Extreme temperature events are increasing in frequency and intensity, yet their effects on autoimmune diseases remain poorly understood. Using nationwide inpatient claims from the Korean National H...

item.title: Frequent exposure to biologics is associated with small intestinal bacterial overgrowth in patients with Crohn's disease: a retrospective case-control study.
item.description: PeerJ - Small intestinal bacterial overgrowth (SIBO) is characterized by excessive bacteria in the small intestine, often accompanied by gastrointestinal symptoms. The advent of biologic therapies has sign...

item.title: Socioeconomic status in Behçet's syndrome.
item.description: Clinical and experimental rheumatology - To compare socioeconomic status (SES) of patients with Behçet's syndrome (BS) with other chronic inflammatory diseases and healthy controls (HC), and to identify associated clinical factors. The In...
